Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition To Answer Questions Pursuant To Pltf'S Deposition Notice And Production Of Documents; Amended Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Dec-02-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Christopher E. Andreas for Plaintiff/Moving Party
Jason G. Gong for Defendants Lincoln & Hobart/Opposing Party

PLAINTIFF BERT VERDUCCI'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION TO ANSWER QUESTIONS PURSUANT TO PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION NOTICE AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS.

HEARING HELD. The motion is granted as it seeks further responses to questions about welding electrodes which contained asbestos and were in use during Plaintiff's work career.
The motion as it relates to questions about warnings, those questions may call for privileged communication, but the response does not establish a foundation for that objection.
The motion is denied as to questions about workman's compensation claims and 401(k) plans as they are irrelevant and no response is required.
The questions about interfacing with counsel in producing document
invade privilege.
The questions about patents are irrelevant, and the motion is denied.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
